bash_commands.command: find /workspace/jdk--742e735d/src -type f -name "*.java" | xargs grep -l "class.*Provider.*extends\|class.*Provider implements" | grep -i "sun\|jce\|jsse\|ec" | head -30
find /workspace/jdk--742e735d/src -type f -name "*.java" | xargs grep -l "class SunJCE\|class SunJSSE\|class Sun " 2>/dev/null | grep -E "(SunJCE|SunJSSE|provider)" | head -20
find /workspace/jdk--742e735d -type f -name "*ProviderConfig*" -o -type f -name "*.java" | xargs grep -l "ProviderConfig" 2>/dev/null | head -20

  "text": "The Java Security Provider registration framework in OpenJDK consists of the following components:\n\n1. **Base Provider Class (java.security.Provider)**: Located at src/java.base/share/classes/java/security/Provider.java, this is the foundation class that all security providers extend. It defines the putService(Service s) method at line 1221, which allows providers to register cryptographic algorithms and services.\n\n2. **Provider Service Registration**: The Provider.Service inner class (defined within Provider.java) represents a service offered by a provider. Providers use putService() to register Service instances for various algorithm types.\n\n3. **JCA Framework - Provider Configuration & Loading**:\n   - **ProviderConfig.java** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/jca/ProviderConfig.java): Implements the lazy-loading mechanism that encapsulates provider configuration (name + optional argument) and provides logic to load Provider objects on-demand. It maps provider names to their implementation classes.\n   - **ProviderList.java** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/jca/ProviderList.java): Manages the ordered list of available providers. It loads providers from security properties and handles provider initialization using ServiceLoader, supporting semi-eager provider creation.\n   - **Providers.java** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/jca/Providers.java): A utility class that manages the system-wide provider list. It contains hardcoded names of key providers (\"SUN\", \"SunRsaSign\", \"SunEC\", \"SunJCE\") and provides access methods like getSunProvider().\n   - **GetInstance.java** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/jca/GetInstance.java): Handles service lookup and instantiation within the provider framework.\n   - **JCAUtil.java** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/jca/JCAUtil.java): Provides JCA-related utility methods.\n   - **ServiceId.java** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/jca/ServiceId.java): Represents service identification within the provider framework.\n\n4. **Core Provider Implementations**:\n   - **Sun Provider** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/provider/Sun.java): The primary provider for core Java Security algorithms including DSA, MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and other basic cryptographic primitives. It extends Provider directly.\n   - **SunEntries.java** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/provider/SunEntries.java): Defines all the service entries (algorithms and implementations) that the Sun provider registers.\n   - **SunJCE Provider** (src/java.base/share/classes/com/sun/crypto/provider/SunJCE.java): Extends Provider to register cryptographic cipher algorithms including AES, DES, DESede, Blowfish, ChaCha20, and various key agreement and key wrap algorithms. It uses helper methods (ps, psA) that call putService() to register Provider.Service instances.\n   - **SunJSSE Provider** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/ssl/SunJSSE.java): Extends Provider to register SSL/TLS protocol factories and implementations.\n   - **SunEC Provider** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/ec/SunEC.java): Extends Provider to register elliptic curve cryptography algorithms including ECDSA, ECDH, EdDSA, and XDH (X25519, X448).\n   - **SunRsaSign Provider** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/rsa/SunRsaSign.java): Extends Provider to register RSA signature algorithms.\n   - **VerificationProvider** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/provider/VerificationProvider.java): A special provider used during JAR verification, extended for signature verification purposes.\n   - **AppleProvider** (src/java.base/macosx/classes/apple/security/AppleProvider.java): Platform-specific provider for macOS that extends Provider to offer system keystore integration.\n\n5. **SSL/TLS Factory Classes** (part of SunJSSE):\n   - **KeyManagerFactoryImpl.java**: Implements KeyManagerFactory for SSL/TLS\n   - **TrustManagerFactoryImpl.java**: Implements TrustManagerFactory for SSL/TLS\n   - **SSLServerSocketFactoryImpl.java**: Implements SSLServerSocketFactory\n   - **SSLSocketFactoryImpl.java**: Implements SSLSocketFactory\n\n**Provider Registration Flow**: When the Java Security system starts, Providers.fromSecurityProperties() initializes ProviderList with ProviderConfig instances for each configured provider. These ProviderConfig objects lazily load the actual Provider implementations (Sun, SunJCE, SunJSSE, SunEC, etc.) on-demand. Each provider registers its services using putService(), making algorithms discoverable via the JCA framework (Security.getInstance())."
}

conversation_events.text:
# OpenJDK Security Provider Registration Framework

## Your Task

Find all Java source files in openjdk/jdk that implement the security provider registration framework. Identify: the base Provider class with putService() (java.security.Provider), the SunJCE provider that registers crypto algorithms, the SunJSSE provider for SSL/TLS factories, the SunEC provider for elliptic curve algorithms, the Sun provider for core algorithms, and the ProviderConfig lazy-loading mechanism that maps provider names to classes.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/jdk--742e735d.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/jdk--742e735d` (openjdk/jdk)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all the Java source files implementing the security provider registration framework in openjdk/jdk. Let me start by exploring the repository structure.

Perfect! I've created a comprehensive analysis of the Java Security Provider registration framework in OpenJDK. The answer file includes:

## Summary of Findings

**Core Components:**
1. **Base Provider Framework** - `java.security.Provider` with `putService()` method for service registration
2. **Lazy-Loading Mechanism** - `ProviderConfig` class that maps provider names to classes with deferred instantiation
3. **Provider List Management** - `ProviderList` and `Providers` classes that manage the ordered list of providers

**Provider Implementations:**
- **Sun Provider** - Core algorithms (DSA, MD5, SHA, etc.)
- **SunJCE Provider** - Cryptographic ciphers (AES, DES, Blowfish, ChaCha20)
- **SunJSSE Provider** - SSL/TLS protocol support with factory classes
- **SunEC Provider** - Elliptic curve algorithms (ECDSA, ECDH, EdDSA, XDH)
- **SunRsaSign Provider** - RSA signature algorithms
- **Platform-specific providers** - AppleProvider for macOS

**Supporting Infrastructure:**
- JCA service lookup mechanism (GetInstance, ServiceId)
- Verification provider for JAR file operations
- SSL/TLS factory implementations

The answer file has been created at `/workspace/answer.json` with complete file listings, key symbols, provider registration chains, and a detailed narrative explanation of how the security provider framework operates.

It defines the putService(Service s) method at line 1221, which allows providers to register cryptographic algorithms and services.\n\n2. **Provider Service Registration**: The Provider.Service inner class (defined within Provider.java) represents a service offered by a provider. Providers use putService() to register Service instances for various algorithm types.\n\n3. **JCA Framework - Provider Configuration & Loading**:\n   - **ProviderConfig.java** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/jca/ProviderConfig.java): Implements the lazy-loading mechanism that encapsulates provider configuration (name + optional argument) and provides logic to load Provider objects on-demand. It maps provider names to their implementation classes.\n   - **ProviderList.java** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/jca/ProviderList.java): Manages the ordered list of available providers. It loads providers from security properties and handles provider initialization using ServiceLoader, supporting semi-eager provider creation.\n   - **Providers.java** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/jca/Providers.java): A utility class that manages the system-wide provider list. It uses helper methods (ps, psA) that call putService() to register Provider.Service instances.\n   - **SunJSSE Provider** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/ssl/SunJSSE.java): Extends Provider to register SSL/TLS protocol factories and implementations.\n   - **SunEC Provider** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/ec/SunEC.java): Extends Provider to register elliptic curve cryptography algorithms including ECDSA, ECDH, EdDSA, and XDH (X25519, X448).\n   - **SunRsaSign Provider** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/rsa/SunRsaSign.java): Extends Provider to register RSA signature algorithms.\n   - **VerificationProvider** (src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/provider/VerificationProvider.java): A special provider used during JAR verification, extended for signature verification purposes.\n   - **AppleProvider** (src/java.base/macosx/classes/apple/security/AppleProvider.java): Platform-specific provider for macOS that extends Provider to offer system keystore integration.\n\n5. **SSL/TLS Factory Classes** (part of SunJSSE):\n   - **KeyManagerFactoryImpl.java**: Implements KeyManagerFactory for SSL/TLS\n   - **TrustManagerFactoryImpl.java**: Implements TrustManagerFactory for SSL/TLS\n   - **SSLServerSocketFactoryImpl.java**: Implements SSLServerSocketFactory\n   - **SSLSocketFactoryImpl.java**: Implements SSLSocketFactory\n\n**Provider Registration Flow**: When the Java Security system starts, Providers.fromSecurityProperties() initializes ProviderList with ProviderConfig instances for each configured provider. Each provider registers its services using putService(), making algorithms discoverable via the JCA framework (Security.getInstance())."
